titleEn: AI crop forecaster takes the next step in scaling up
titleZh: AI作物产量预测器迈出规模化新一步
summaryZh: Polybee与拜耳达成商业合作，将其AI驱动的作物产量预测技术通过拜耳的客户关系网络规模化推广。该技术通过无人机和AI实现精准收获时机推荐，已在菠菜和西兰花上验证可带来8%-15%的产量提升，并计划扩展至生菜等作物。Polybee采用按公顷固定收费模式，强调快速投资回报，并计划通过非排他性合作拓展全球市场。
translationZh:
Polybee表示，其与拜耳的新商业合作标志着其AI驱动的作物产量预测技术规模化推广的下一阶段，此前该公司已用多年时间证明该平台能为种植者带来可衡量的回报。

该公司最近宣布与拜耳达成协议，通过拜耳现有的客户关系网络扩大其产量预测技术的覆盖范围。据Polybee创始人兼CEO Siddharth Jadhav称，这一合作是长期关系的成果，而非一笔简单的商业交易。

“我们对自己正在打造的产品有着非常清晰的北极星目标，那就是通过可视化和控制力来提高新鲜农产品的利润，”Jadhav说。“利用无人机和AI进行产量预测，以及自主无人机授粉，都是具体的应用，但它们都有一个非常明确的目标：增强可视化和控制力，从而提升利润。”

在追求大规模扩张之前，Polybee专注于为种植者展示快速的回报。

“我们按每公顷收取固定费用，包含所有设备、软件、安装、维护和支持。当收获时机建议被采纳后，我们的客户在同一作物周期内就能获得三倍、五倍甚至七倍的回报，”他说。“你不应该等上好几年才能看到回报。”

证明技术有效性的过程经过了刻意严苛的测试。Polybee让早期客户对整个生产区域进行预测，然后要求他们仅对其中一半区域按照建议进行操作。

“假设有100公顷，”Jadhav说。“在这100公顷中，我们对每一英亩都给出预测。他们会对其中一半区域按照这些预测和收获日期建议进行操作。另一半则照常管理。”

在不同品种、季节和农场位置的对比测试中，仅通过更精准地安排收获时间，且不增加种植者的边际成本，就实现了8%到15%的产量提升。

“当你获得这样的收益时，很难忽视它，”他说。“主角是种植者。我们都只是配角。我们提出的任何商业模式都需要体现这一点。”

Polybee决定与已经与种植者建立了信任关系的成熟农业合作伙伴合作，最初瞄准他们的重点客户。

“我们是一家非常年轻的公司，而我们的潜在市场是全球性的，”他说。“我们无法独自完成这一切。我们需要相信产品、相信我们为客户创造的ROI，并与我们并肩作战的渠道合作伙伴。”

Jadhav表示，与拜耳的协议是非排他性的，公司可以自由地在其他地方建立类似的合作关系。

“对我们来说，这是通过已有的信任关系接触更多客户的一种方式。这是我们尊重农业作为一门生意本质的体现。”

这段关系还帮助Polybee扩展到了最初专注的水果作物之外。

“拜耳向我们指出了在蔬菜作物上进行产量预测的机会，”他解释道。“我们最初非常专注于水果作物，但他们告诉我们还有其他作物。”

这促成了在菠菜和西兰花上的试点，如今这两个作物是该产品已验证的领域。拜耳在此之前很早就支持了公司的早期发展，介绍潜在客户并提供关于行业认可标准的反馈。

“即使在产品尚未成熟时，他们就已经通过引荐展现了投入，”他说。“当他们看到我们开发出了产品并成功销售时，我们才坐到一起，决定签署协议。”

下一个作物是生菜，正在开发中，预计年底前推出，而客户一直在问Polybee为什么不从生菜开始。

“许多客户来找我们说，嘿，你们为什么不从生菜开始？因为那才是我们的摇钱树。我认为这是迈向更大机遇的第一步，”Jadhav说。“我们想证明，如果以正确的商业模式打造正确的产品，最终每个人都会成为赢家。”
featuredReason: 该新闻展示了AI与无人机技术在精准农业中的成熟应用，通过非排他性合作模式实现规模化，对智慧畜牧行业（尤其是种植业）的技术落地和商业模式创新具有重要参考价值。

 Monday, 20 July 2026
 

 
 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 
 Siddharth Jadhav - Polybee

 AI crop forecaster takes the next step in scaling up

 

 
 Polybee says its new commercial partnership with Bayer marks the next stage in scaling its AI-powered crop forecasting technology, following years of proving that the platform can deliver measurable returns for growers.

 The company recently announced an agreement with Bayer to expand access to its yield forecasting technology through Bayer's existing customer relationships. According to Polybee founder and CEO Siddharth Jadhav, the partnership is the result of a long-term relationship rather than a single commercial deal.

 © Polybee

 "We have a very clear North Star with the product that we are building, which is to increase profits in fresh produce through visibility and control," Jadhav says. "Yield forecasting with drones and AI and autonomous drone pollination are applications, but they all have a very clear objective of enhancing visibility and control and therefore enhancing profits."

 Before pursuing large-scale expansion, Polybee focused on demonstrating a rapid return on investment for growers.

 "We charge a fixed fee per hectare which includes everything: equipment, software, setup, maintenance and support. When harvest timing recommendations are followed, our customers walk away with three, five or even seven times return in the very same crop cycle," he says. "You shouldn't have to wait for years until that return is realised."

 © Polybee

 Proving the tech worked took a deliberately unforgiving test. Polybee gave early customers forecasts across their entire production, then asked them to act on the recommendations for only half of it.

 "Let's say it's 100 hectares," Jadhav says. "Out of those 100 hectares, we were giving them forecasts on every single acre. They would follow those forecasts and recommendations for harvest dates on half of it. The other half would be business as usual."

 Run across varieties, times of year and farm locations, the comparison produced a consistent 8 to 15 per cent yield uplift, achieved purely by timing the harvest more precisely and at no marginal cost to the grower.

 "When you're getting that kind of benefit, it's hard to ignore," he says. "The protagonist is the grower. We are all just playing a supporting cast. Whatever business model we come up with needs to reflect that."

 Polybee decided to work with established agricultural partners that already have trusted relationships with growers, initially targeting their key accounts.

 "We're a very young company and our addressable market is global," he says. "We cannot do that all alone. We are going to need channel partners who believe in the product, believe in the ROI we're creating for customers, and work with us shoulder to shoulder."

 Jadhav says the agreement with Bayer is non-exclusive, leaving the company free to establish similar partnerships elsewhere.

 "For us, this is a way to get to more customers through trusted relationships that already exist. This is us respecting the nature of agriculture as a business."

 The relationship also helped Polybee expand beyond its original focus on fruit crops.

 "Bayer pointed us to the opportunity of doing yield forecasting in vegetable crops," he explains. "We initially had a very tight focus on fruit crops, but they told us there were these other crops too."

 That led to pilots in spinach and broccoli, the two crops the product is proven in today. Bayer had supported the company's early development well before that point, introducing potential customers and providing feedback on what the industry would accept as proven.

 "They showed skin in the game by making introductions even when the product was not ready," he says. "When they saw we had built the product and were successfully selling it, that's when we got to the table and decided to ink something together."

 Lettuce is next, in the works and expected by the end of the year, and customers have been asking why Polybee did not start there.

 "Many of our customers came to us and said, hey, why did you not start with lettuce? Because that's really the moneymaker for us. I do see this as the first step towards a much bigger opportunity," Jadhav says. "We want to demonstrate that if the right product is built with the right business model, everyone ends up on the winning side."

 For more information:Siddharth JadhavPolybeehttps://polybee.co/ [email protected]
